---
title: Publishing Agents
description: "Publish or unpublish an agent in the UI or via the API, find draft agents, and understand what changes when you do."
---

import { Aside, Steps, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components'

A new agent starts as a **draft**: visible only to you and admin-tier roles.
Publishing makes it visible and usable for everyone.
For the full visibility model and permission matrices, see [identity and permissions](/agent-studio-docs/security/identity-and-permissions/#permission-matrices).

## Publish or unpublish in the UI

<Steps>
1. **Open your agent** in Agent Studio.

2. **Locate the publish controls** in the top-right corner of the agent page.
   - Draft agents show a **Publish** button.
   - Published agents show a **Published** badge and an **Unpublish** button.

   ![Draft agent with Publish button](../../../../assets/img/guides/access-controls/agent-publish-button.png)

   ![Published agent with Unpublish button](../../../../assets/img/guides/access-controls/agent-published-status.png)

3. **Click the button** to toggle the published status.
   - **Publish** makes the agent visible to all users.
   - **Unpublish** returns it to draft, hiding it from non-admin users.
</Steps>

<Aside type="caution">
If you unpublish an agent that others are using, they lose access immediately.
The agent remains visible only in your Draft tab and to admin-tier roles.
</Aside>

## Finding draft agents

Your draft agents appear in the **Draft** tab when browsing agents.
Other users' drafts are not visible to you unless you are a Server Admin or Catalog Admin.

![Agent browse view with Draft tab](../../../../assets/img/guides/access-controls/agent-browse-draft-tab.png)

<Aside type="tip" title="Can't find your custom agents?">
Custom agents created before the publish/draft feature was introduced are now in draft status.
Check the **Draft** tab, and publish them to make them visible to other users again.
</Aside>

## Publishing via the API (alternative)

You can also change an agent's published status programmatically with a `PATCH` request:

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="Bearer token">
```bash
curl -X PATCH 'https://<your-tenant>.alationcloud.com/ai/api/v1/config/agent/<agent-id>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <your-oauth-token>' \
  -d '{"published_status": "published"}'
```
</TabItem>
<TabItem label="curl (session)">
```bash
curl -X PATCH 'https://<your-tenant>.alationcloud.com/ai/api/v1/config/agent/<agent-id>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'x-csrftoken: <your-csrf-token>' \
  -H 'Cookie: sessionid=<your-session-id>; csrftoken=<your-csrf-token>' \
  -d '{"published_status": "published"}'
```
</TabItem>
</Tabs>

To unpublish, send `{"published_status": "draft"}`.

The agent ID is in the URL when viewing the agent (`/app/studio/agents/a/<agent-id>`), or list your agents with `GET /ai/api/v1/config/agent`.

**Who can change published status:** standard roles and above, for their own agents.
Only Server Admin can change other users' agents.
Viewer and Explorer roles cannot publish (they cannot edit agents at all).

## Related

- [Identity and permissions](/agent-studio-docs/security/identity-and-permissions/#permission-matrices) — the visibility model and full permission matrices.
- [Publish an agent to MCP](/agent-studio-docs/integrations/mcp/using-the-mcp-server/#publish-an-agent-to-mcp) — expose the published agent as a tool for MCP clients and other agents.
